task_name: Surgical Simulation Lab Tour and Program Overview
reasoning: This video is an instructional/promotional tour of a surgical simulation laboratory facility. It contains narrated overviews of equipment (daVinci console, Simbionix trainer, FLS trainer setup), facility capabilities, and program information. There is no actual performance of an FLS task visible. The frames show laboratory tours, equipment demonstrations, instructor introductions, and facility descriptions — not a trainee or expert performing a timed FLS task (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture).

teaching_content.facility_overview: University of Arizona Surgical Education & Simulation Laboratory (Room 4086) — comprehensive training center for residents
equipment_available: Complete daVinci robotic surgical console with separate coach monitor
FLS (Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery) trainers — multiple tasks
Simbionix simulation platform for straight laparoscopic training
da Vinci robots for operative room practice
VA facility with simulator upgrade for off-hours practice
program_benefits: Surgical simulation training improves resident skill development
Surgeons with gaming history learn faster
Supervised training sessions with surgical educator Dr. Adam available
Multiple modalities: robotic, FLS, and straight laparoscopic training
Progress monitoring and skill tracking via accounts
instructional_cues: Practice until suturing is smooth, precise, and efficient
Senior residents can warm up before big cases
Robot practice in OR for docking, arm adjustment, instrument passage
Individual instruction available for specific skills
teaching_content.target_audience: Surgical residents — training program overview and encouragement
